stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Flamingo missiles hit more far-flung Russian targets
stories.summary: Military factory in Cheboksary lies 900km from frontline; Zelenskyy inaugurates ‘Day of the Unmanned Systems Forces’. Volodymyr Zelenskyy, Ukraine’s president, said Ukrainian FP-5 Flamingo long-range missiles hit a military factory in Cheboksary that supplies components for Russian drones and missiles. It is located in the Chuvashiya region more than 900km (560 miles) from the frontline. The Astra

stories.title: Poll: Voter cynicism remains a potent threat to incumbents across the globe
stories.summary: Voters punished ruling parties across the globe in 2024. They are doing it again now. The same voters who rejected their rulers without mercy on both sides of the Atlantic — throwing out Britain’s Conservatives after 14 years in power and humbling Democrats in the United States — are now poised to deliver resounding defeats to the very leaders they elected two years ago. stories.summary: People have absorbed violence and terror into their lives. I’d just eaten fried red mullet from the Black Sea on a pavement terrace, listening to the cries of the last swifts as darkness crept over the city. stories.title: Céges számlanyitás az irodából: elég a szelfi a magyar bankoknak?
stories.summary: A legegyszerűbb dolog lenne egy cégvezető életében, amennyiben új bankszámlát szeretne nyitni a vállalkozásának, ha hátradőlne a székében és akár a mobiljával elintézné ezt percek alatt. Ez azonban ma még jószerivel csak álom. A BiztosDöntés.hu összegyűjtötte, melyik banknál milyen módon hozhatnak létre pénzforgalmi számlát a vállalkozók a bankfiókon kívül.

stories.title: A hűtőtáskás rántott hús a múlté, a nyaralóba érkezik a grillezni való (x)
stories.summary: A balatoni nyár ma nem olyan, mint régen. Gyerekkorunkban már az indulás előtti este elkezdődött egy kisebb logisztikai hadművelet, majd a csomagtartóba és a hátsó ülésre került a fél háztartás, és esetleg még mi, gyerekek is befértünk az autóba a pokrócok, strandlabdák és gumimatracok közé. Lábunkat a hűtőtáskára helyeztük, amiben természetesen mindig ott lapult jó néhány rántott húsos szendvics. Ma már azonban a magyar tenger körül is gyorsan a nyaralóba rendelhetjük a legfrissebb termékeket.

stories.summary: Capping the population at 10 million is a far-right fantasy. stories.summary: The final conclusions of the investigation have yet to be published, although more could become apparent in the coming days.

stories.title: Watch: Historic US-Canada border library gets new Quebec-only entrance
stories.summary: After more than a century of sharing the main entrance, Canadians were barred from using the library's US-side door by the Trump administration in 2025.

stories.title: What we learned from Bill Gates' deposition on Epstein

stories.title: Iran says ceasefire now 'practically meaningless' after US strikes
stories.summary: After a second day of exchange of fire between the US and Iran, Tehran further said the Strait of Hormuz was now shut to all shipping, a claim CENTCOM denied.

stories.title: Family of British toddler criticises police as Australian inquiry into cold case murders begins
stories.summary: NSW inquiry will also examine whether notorious serial killer Ivan Milat was responsible for more murders.

stories.title: Knicks fans go wild as New York team makes biggest comeback in NBA Finals history
stories.summary: Wednesday's result means the team are now just one win away from a famous Finals win.

stories.title: Spain: Pope Leo XIV blesses new Sagrada Familia tower, now world’s tallest church
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV blesses Sagrada Familia’s new tower in Barcelona, now the world’s tallest church, in a mass marking 100 years since Antoni Gaudí’s death.

stories.title: Thai court sentences two men to death over Bangkok shrine bombing
stories.summary: Twenty people died and 120 others were injured in the explosion at the Erawan shrine 11 years ago.

stories.title: Who is Jared Kushner - the man funding Albania's controversial property deal?
stories.summary: The White House envoy has long attracted attention for his links to US politics and his business dealings around the world, but as controversy grows over a property deal in Albania, many are asking: Who is Jared Kushner?

stories.title: Haiti embraces World Cup dream after 54-year wait
stories.summary: Haiti's Ti Mondial 2026 tournament brings together 48 schools to celebrate the World Cup and promote peace, unity and hope.

stories.title: A sneak peek inside Mexico’s iconic Azteca Stadium before World Cup kickoff
stories.summary: The BBC's Will Grant got access inside the renovated venue ahead of the first game of the tournament.

stories.title: French singer Patrick Bruel under formal investigation for rape
stories.summary: Bruel was released from custody under judicial supervision while an examining magistrate looks more deeply into the case.

stories.title: Trump says 'I love the inflation' as US prices rise at fastest rate in three years
stories.summary: The US president later said he had meant that he actually loved that inflation was not higher.

stories.title: Pope Leo visits Canary Islands to highlight perilous journeys of migrants
stories.summary: During his trip, the Pope will appeal for a humane approach and respectful welcome for migrants seeking a better life.

stories.title: US strikes Iran-bound tanker near Oman, sparking India protest over missing crew
stories.summary: US Central Command said it fired on the Palau-flagged Settebello after the crew ignored orders, hitting its engine room as the vessel attempted to carry Iranian oil in violation of the US blockade.

stories.title: UK: Belfast violence leaves homes burnt and families displaced
stories.summary: Belfast awoke to scenes of destruction on 10 June after anti immigration protests descended into violence across several parts of Northern Ireland, leaving homes damaged, vehicles burnt out and families displaced.

stories.title: Deadly clashes in Kenya as protesters fight US Ebola quarantine facility
stories.summary: Kenya saw fresh unrest on 9 June as protesters defied a court order and clashed with police near Laikipia Air Base over plans for a US funded Ebola quarantine facility, fuelling fears about the virus and prompting arrests.

stories.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17, reports say
stories.summary: Nine of them were killed in a series of attacks in the town of Tayr Debba, according to Lebanon's state news agency.

stories.title: Pakistan launches deadly air strikes in Afghanistan, reigniting tensions
stories.summary: The fresh strikes come after weeks of relative calm in the restive border region between the two countries.

stories.title: Trump says 'I love the inflation,' claims US taking millions of barrels of Iranian oil
stories.summary: President Donald Trump welcomed new inflation data showing prices rising 4.2%, saying the numbers were "great." He argued higher energy costs linked to the Iran conflict were temporary and claimed US forces were moving millions of barrels of oil through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: 'Children are dying,' Cuba says as US blockade hampers delivery of UN aid
stories.summary: The blockade, coupled with expanded US sanctions that punish companies doing business with the Cuban state, has intensified the worst economic and energy crisis on the island in over a generation.

stories.title: Man shot dead during protest against proposed US Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya
stories.summary: Police dispersed demonstrators in Nanyuki, 120 miles from Nairobi, amid rising anger at US plans Kenyan police have shot dead a man during a protest against a proposed Ebola quarantine facility for US citizens. Patrick Wahome, who has organised protests in Nanyuki against the centre, told Reuters on Tuesday the man died from a gunshot wound to the head. Reporters from the agency saw his body lying motionless in a police van with a large head wound. Continue reading...

stories.title: Putin and Trump are both trapped in losing battles against reality | Rafael Behr
stories.summary: The Ukraine and Iran wars are very different, but a common authoritarian delusion unites the men who started them A strongman president, self-styled redeemer of national glory, is trapped in a conflict he can’t win but doesn’t know how to end without looking like a loser. A cult of infallibility prevents the leader admitting a strategic blunder even to himself. It could be Donald Trump or Vladimir Putin; Iran or Ukraine. The conflicts and the regimes involved are also dissimilar in important way

stories.title: Two killed in rare street demonstration over women’s rights in Afghanistan
stories.summary: A child reported among those killed when Taliban forces fired on crowds in Herat, who were protesting over arrests of women accused of violating hijab dress code A Taliban crackdown on women’s dress code in Afghanistan has escalated into a rare mass street protest in the western province of Herat, with at least two people, including one boy, killed by security forces. Officials made a wave of arrests in recent days targeting women and young girls accused of “improper hijab”. Residents say many f

stories.title: Northern Ireland: Police urge calm after 'sickening' Belfast stabbing
stories.summary: Belfast police appealed for calm after a violent knife attack caught on videophone prompted calls for anti-immigration protests. This followed a week of migration-related riots last June in Northern Ireland.

stories.title: War in Ukraine: Kyiv strikes key Russian supply lines
stories.summary: Russian troops rely on supplies via the land corridor to Crimea, but Ukrainian forces are now able to launch concentrated attacks along the critical route. Experts say this indicates growing Russian vulnerability.

stories.title: US strikes Iran in 'proportional response' to US Apache helicopter crash
stories.summary: A military response from the US could threaten the ceasefire that has been in place since 8 April, as Tehran warned "foreign forces" to leave the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump targeting immigrants from countries hit most by climate shocks
stories.summary: A Guardian analysis reveals how most of 39 countries facing US entry restrictions are most vulnerable environmentally ‘Every day it’s more barriers’: how the US is shutting out climate refugees Donald Trump ’s immigration crackdown is largely targeting people from the countries most vulnerable to displacement from climate-driven disasters, a Guardian analysis shows. As the Trump administration pushes policies to boost planet-heating fossil fuels , millions of people are being forced to flee thei

stories.title: Four days of extreme rain in Indonesia killed 7% of world’s rarest great apes, study finds
stories.summary: Critically endangered Tapanuli orangutan population falls after heavy rain and landslides, fuelled by climate crisis, in North Sumatra Extreme rainfall and landslides fuelled by the climate crisis killed 7% of the remaining population of the world’s rarest great ape, a study has found, prompting fears for the species’ survival. The research suggests 58 out of the remaining 800 critically endangered Tapanuli orangutans ( Pongo tapanuliensis ) were killed after more than 1,000mm (39in) of rain fel

stories.title: Crowds turn out in Barcelona for pope blessing Sagrada Família’s final tower
stories.summary: As completion of 144-year basilica nears, questions swirl over resemblance of church to architect’s original plans It has been a long wait but 144 years after work began, Pope Leo XIV has blessed the recently completed central tower of Antoni Gaudí’s Sagrada Família church in the presence of members of the Spanish royal family, the prime minister and hundreds of bishops. With the completion of the Jesus Christ tower, the tallest of 18 in the temple, the basilica has reached its full height of 17

stories.title: The strongest El Nino in more than a century may be coming
stories.summary: Warnings of a powerful El Nino weather pattern this year suggest that we may be about to face drought, flooding and extreme heat. What impacts are in store? And can we prepare for the worst of it?

stories.title: Neo-Nazi lost an east German election, but extremism remains
stories.summary: A neo-Nazi candidate surprisingly lost a mayoral election by a small margin in the eastern state of Saxony. Many are relieved. But one win doesn't make the extremism problem go away.

stories.title: India news: Modi becomes longest-serving elected PM
stories.summary: Narendra Modi has become the longest-serving elected prime minister of India.

stories.title: Philippines: Farmers race to keep up with global 'ube' craze
stories.summary: Ube, also called the purple yam, went from a nostalgic treat in the Philippines to an exotic item in cafes around the world. But the popularity means the crop supply is under pressure.

stories.title: Rising cyberscam losses expose gaps in EU response
stories.summary: Europeans are losing billions to online scams, yet the EU has been slower than the US, the UK and China in targeting the Southeast Asian networks behind many of them.

stories.title: Why is Hezbollah still important for Iran?
stories.summary: Although weakened, the Lebanon-based Hezbollah militant group remains a central piece of Iran's power projection in the Middle East. Tehran has insisted they be included in any peace deal with the US and Israel.

stories.title: Only 1 in 10 Europeans see US as an ally — study
stories.summary: Europeans' trust in the United States has plummeted since Donald Trump returned to the White House. Most Europeans also don't think the US would help if the continent were attacked.

stories.title: World Cup 2026: How an old song from Bosnia went viral
stories.summary: Some have already called it the unofficial song of the 2026 World Cup. So how did a tune that was more than a decade old take the Balkans — and beyond — by storm?

stories.title: Bill Gates tells Epstein panel he 'never victimized anyone'
stories.summary: The Microsoft co-founder and billionaire philanthropist Bill Gates voluntarily appeared before the House Oversight Committee. He said Jeffrey Epstein was "working to use information" about his extramarital affairs.

stories.title: Middle East: US says it completed latest attacks on Iran
stories.summary: US Central Command had said it hit new targets in Iran in response to Tehran's "unwarranted and continued aggression." Iranian state media reported blasts in several parts of the country. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Manhunt under way in South Africa after 12 killed in mass shooting in Johannesburg
stories.summary: Another nine people were injured at an informal settlement in Cleveland late on Tuesday.

stories.title: Netherlands: Marktweg in The Hague becomes iconic orange street for World Cup fever
stories.summary: Residents of Marktweg in The Hague have once again transformed their street into a sea of orange ahead of the FIFA World Cup, continuing a popular local tradition that has become a symbol of community spirit and support for the national team.

stories.title: Philippines continues rescue effort after powerful Mindanao quake
stories.summary: The Philippines continued rescue operations on 10 June after a magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck Mindanao, killing at least 37 people, injuring nearly 500 and forcing more than 32,000 residents from their homes.

stories.title: Air India crash pilot's father vows to defend son's reputation
stories.summary: Investigators are expected to soon release an update on the crash, which killed 260 people last year.

stories.title: Thai woman in custody after US diplomat found dead in Myanmar, State Department says
stories.summary: The Thai Embassy in Yangon and Thailand's Foreign Ministry refused to say whether they had provided consular support to the suspect or give any other information.

stories.title: First charges laid over deadly Hong Kong fire
stories.summary: The Wang Fuk Court blaze last year was the deadliest that Hong Kong had seen in 70 years, killing 168 people.

stories.title: Hong Kong charges seven people and two firms over massive fire that killed 168
stories.summary: The massive blaze, which engulfed seven of the eight high-rise apartment blocks at the Wang Fuk Court estate in November, prompted a months-long investigation into the cause.

stories.title: Growing backlash in Japan over Trump's use of anime characters
stories.summary: The US president has angered some fans by using the images of characters like Pikachu and Naruto.

stories.title: US official claims banned referee had links with 'terror organisations'
stories.summary: Somali referee Omar Artan was denied entry to the United States because of his "association with suspected members of terror organisations," says a US official.

stories.title: Multiple attackers kill 12 people in late-night shooting in South Africa, police say
stories.summary: Police said the motive was not yet known and no arrests had been made, but there were suspicions it was related to competition over illegal mining in the area.

stories.title: Norway crown princess's son to stay in custody before rape verdict, says court
stories.summary: Thr ruling overturns a lower court's decision on Monday ordering the release of the crown princess's 29-year-old son.

stories.title: 'I will never get over watching my home burn': Belfast residents reel after night of unrest
stories.summary: A night of disorder in Belfast saw people forced to flee their homes, in protests that followed a knife attack in the north of the city.

stories.title: Whale graveyard dating back five million years discovered
stories.summary: The Indian Ocean site is "far beyond anything we had imagined", one researcher says.

stories.title: Bill Gates says Epstein wanted personal relationship, but he 'never reciprocated'
stories.summary: The Microsoft co-founder spent hours talking to lawmakers behind closed doors, where he also said Epstein used his marital infidelities to pressure him.

stories.title: Infantino defends World Cup ticket prices, says fans 'should chill' about ref denied US entry
stories.summary: The 2026 men's World Cup has been expanded to 48 teams and is the biggest and most ambitious in the event's history, with matches taking place in Mexico, Canada and the United States.

stories.title: Somalia's World Cup referee returns home to hero's welcome after US entry denial
stories.summary: Somali referee Omar Artan was welcomed by hundreds of supporters in Mogadishu after being denied entry to the United States and removed from the 2026 World Cup officials list. Artan vowed to return for the next tournament.

stories.title: Fireworks illuminate Barcelona's Sagrada Família during Pope visit
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV says Barcelona's iconic Sagrada Família is a masterpiece of "stones, colours and light" during his visit to Spain.

stories.title: An Everest guide's miraculous survival raises questions for tourism industry
stories.summary: Why was a cook leading clients up the world's highest peak? And why was he left to rescue himself?

stories.title: 11+7 meccs, amit ne hagyjon ki a futball-világbajnokságon
stories.summary: Angol–horvát csúcsrangadó? Az aranyesélyes brazilok a vb egyik leggyengébb csapata ellen? Messiék egy taktikai zsenivel szemben? Mi ezeket nem hagynánk ki, de bőven lesz olyan meccs, amiért az éjszaka közepén is érdemes lehet felkelni.

stories.title: Egészen az ötvenes évekig bort kaptak a francia gyerekek az ebédjük mellé az iskolában
stories.summary: Nem azért szolgáltak fel alkoholt a gyerekeknek, hogy elbódítsák őket, hanem mert valóban hittek benne, hogy egészséges, és a szokás fontos gasztrokulturális gesztus is lehetett.

stories.summary: Az amerikai erők légvédelmi rendszereket támadtak meg, Irán válaszul amerikai bázisokra lőtt az Öbölben.

stories.title: Megszereztük a 25 évre szóló, ezermilliárd forintos megállapodást, amit a választás előtt írt alá Krausz Ferenc és Hankó Balázs
stories.summary: Korábban csak egy hatéves szerződés volt ismert a Nobel-díjas tudós vezette Élvonal Alapítvány és a Kulturális és Innovációs Minisztérium között. A Telex most közérdekűadat-igényléssel megszerezte a 2050-ig szóló keretmegállapodást – amiben egy további 25 éves opció is van.

stories.title: Az NBA-döntők történetének legnagyobb fordítását vitte véghez a New York Knicks
stories.summary: A San Antonio Spurs 29 ponttal vezetett, de ebből a hátrányból is visszakapaszkodott a Knicks, és 1,2 másodperccel a vége előtt fordított.

stories.summary: Új eszközt adna a Tisza a közpénzes magántőkealapok befolyásolására, 75 százalékról 60-ra csökkenhet a jóváhagyási küszöb fontos döntéseknél.

stories.title: Indul az Ünnepi Könyvhét, keresd a Telex standját és programjait!
stories.summary: Kedvezményes árakkal, új termékekkel, könyvbemutatókkal és dedikálásokkal várunk mindenkit szeretettel a Vörösmarty téren.

stories.summary: A közlekedési és beruházási miniszter azt mondta, Lázár Jánostól egy felperzselt földet örökölt, elődje stábjára nem támaszkodik.

stories.title: Hosszas huzavona után megállapodott a Mol és Szerbia a NIS megvásárlásáról
stories.summary: A Mol és a szerb állam minden nyitott kérdést le tudott zárni a határidő előtt.

stories.summary: Szíriába és Ukrajnába is több ember tért haza, mint korábban.

stories.title: Reagált a FIFA elnöke a világbajnokság kritikáira: Csak nyugodjatok meg, lazuljatok el!
stories.summary: Gianni Infantino a nyitómeccs előtti sajtótájékoztatón azt javasolta az újságíróknak, hogy a torna alatt a futballra koncentráljanak.

stories.title: Vezetőcsere jön az MFB élén, Gerendás János váltja Kovács Zsoltot
stories.summary: Gerendás korábban az MNB monetáris politikai, majd statisztikai igazgatója volt.

stories.title: Növénymakró? Horgászszék? Talpas pohár? Nem kötelező, de jól jöhet
stories.summary: Itt a Karakter heti programajánlója.

stories.title: Heteken belül jön a Temu-vám, júliustól három eurót kell fizetni a kisebb rendelések után
stories.summary: A vámot nem csomagonként, hanem az egy csomagban található termékkategóriák után kell megfizetni.

stories.summary: Több forrás szerint az amerikai elnök Washingtonban marad pénteken, helyette Marco Rubio külügyminiszter találkozik majd a paraguayi elnökkel Los Angelesben.

stories.title: A spanyolok és a franciák a vb esélyesei
stories.summary: A fogadóirodák oddsai alapján a spanyol és a francia válogatottnak van a legnagyobb esélye megnyerni a ma kezdődő labdarúgó-világbajnokságot.

stories.title: Kódszavakkal beszélgetnek pedofilok kiskorúak TikTok-videóinál
stories.summary: Egy spanyol tényellenőrző csoport kutatása azt mutatja, hogy pedofilok a TikTokról indulva építenek ki kapcsolatot egymással, vagy próbálnak gyerekeket más platformra terelni.

stories.title: Karácsony Gergely szerint pénzkidobás volt, ha lefizetett valakit a fővárosnál a parkmaffia vezetője

stories.summary: Ben Cohen szerint az új tulajdonos nem engedi, hogy kritizálják Donald Trumpot.

stories.title: Az amerikai egyetemi bajnokságban megdöntötték a 110 gát 14 éve fennálló világcsúcsát
stories.summary: A 20 éves Ja'Kobe Tharp hatalmasat javított egyéni csúcsán az elődöntőben, a korábbi világrekordnál 5 századdal volt gyorsabb.

stories.summary: Nádas 1973-ban fogott hozzá az Emlékiratok könyvéhez, amelynek első kéziratát megsemmisítette.

stories.title: Oroszország fiatal ukrán nőket bérelt fel ukrán katonák meggyilkolására
stories.summary: Az ukránok elkaptak egy 17 évest, aki méreggel próbált megölni egy katonát.

stories.title: Japanese manga fans urge Trump to stop using characters in his online posts
stories.summary: Renewed outrage at White House’s use manga and anime imagery after US president is depicted as ninja Naruto Japanese anime and manga fans are urging Donald Trump to stop using their favourite characters in his social media posts. About 20,000 people have signed a petition on Change.org entitled Protect Japanese Manga, protesting against the official White House X account posting videos featuring unauthorised use of imagery from the popular Dragon Ball, Yu-Gi-Oh! and Naruto series. Angry fans hav

stories.title: EU proposes entry ban for Russians who fought in Ukraine
stories.summary: The EU's 21st sanctions package includes a provision to deny entry to anyone who has served in Russia's armed forces amid the invasion of Ukraine. It will also target the so-called shadow fleet, banks and cryptocurrency.

stories.title: 'Disclosure Day': For Spielberg, aliens are not just fiction
stories.summary: As Steven Spielberg releases a new film on extraterrestrials, here's a brief history of modern alien conspiracies and why scientists are now taking UFO reports seriously.

stories.title: Israeli warnings trigger mass exodus from Lebanon’s historic Tyre
stories.summary: Thousands of residents fled Lebanon's southern city of Tyre on 9 June, with long queues of vehicles heading north after Israeli military warnings heightened fears of further air strikes and a wider regional escalation.

stories.title: Tehran fires missiles at Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain after renewed US strikes on Iran
stories.summary: Jordan intercepted five missiles aimed at an area hosting a major airbase near Azraq while Kuwait and Bahrain said their air defences were engaging incoming fire. The attacks followed US strikes on Iranian air defence and radar sites near the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Cars and homes burn as unrest erupts in Belfast after knife attack
stories.summary: Multiple fires burned across parts of Belfast as anti-immigration protests erupted following a stabbing attack. Footage showed burning vehicles, damaged homes and emergency crews responding overnight.

stories.title: Former Air Canada pilot charged after allegedly flying without proper license for 16 years
stories.summary: Geoffrey Wall is alleged to have flown over 900 flights domestically and internationally between 2009 and 2025 A former Air Canada pilot has been charged after flying for years without a proper license, Canadian police have said. Geoffrey Wall, of Barrie, Ontario, is alleged to have operated as an airline captain between 2009 and 2025 without a license to fly large commercial passenger planes, according to Peel regional police. Continue reading...

stories.title: Global brands ‘likely’ using mineral that funds rebels accused of atrocities in DRC, investigation finds
stories.summary: Amazon and Sony among firms that may have sourced coltan, used in phones, from supply chains controlled by the M23 rebels, says Global Witness Leading global brands including Amazon, Ericsson and Sony are “likely” to have sourced minerals linked to a militia accused of widespread sexual violence, summary executions and torture, a new investigation claims. The companies allegedly, but unknowingly, acquired coltan smuggled from mines in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) that are occupied

stories.title: The vanishing of Nicolás Maduro: how the former dictator is being erased from Venezuela
stories.summary: Billboards are being painted over and former allies seem eager to forget the man they once glorified For years, his bewhiskered face stared down from propaganda billboards glorifying the supposedly revolutionary rule of a dictator who styled himself as “the protector of the people”. The spin-doctored adoration was such that factories churned out plastic action figures exalting Nicolás Maduro as an “indestructible” and “iron-fisted” caped crusader nicknamed “Super Moustache”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Százmilliókat fizethetnek be ezek a települések a költségvetésbe
stories.summary: A Közlönyben megjelent, kinek mennyit kell perkálnia a helyi iparűzési adóbevétel többlete alapján.

stories.title: Forradalmasították a fogyást, de van, akinél durva folyamatokat indíthatnak be az új szerek
stories.summary: Az éhségérzetet elnyomó fogyasztószerek némelyeknél már túlzott fogyást okoznak, az étkezési zavarokkal küszködőknél pedig akár veszélyes és kóros ételelutasítást is beindíthatnak.

stories.title: Biró Ferencre letöltendő börtönbüntetést és 23 milliós vagyonelkobzást kért az ügyészség

stories.title: Gumicsizmában futott a betonon Székelyvarság polgármestere a medve elől
stories.summary: Eredetileg egy csikót próbált elejteni az állat, később megkergette a kiérkező hatóságokat.

stories.title: Felgyújtott házak, betört ablakok, városszerte lángoló autók
stories.summary: Egy szudáni menekült hétfő este a nyílt utcán próbált késsel brutálisan megölni egy férfit Belfastban. A történtek után kedd este elszabadult a káosz az északír városban. A durva bevándorlóellenes zavargások után már az áldozat családja intett nyugalomra. A kormányfő szerint felelősségre vonják az elkövetőket, és hagyni kell, hogy a rendőrség végezze a munkáját.

stories.title: A kormány leszedeti a nemzeti hitvallás szövegét a közintézményekben, megszűnik a kriptovaluták kriminalizációja
stories.summary: A kormány egy sor kérdésben döntött a szerdai kormányülésen.

stories.title: Kapitány István új részleteket jelentett be az 500 milliárdos EU-s energetikai fejlesztésekről
stories.summary: Okosmérők telepítése és nagyszabású hálózatfejlesztés is szerepel a tervek között.

stories.title: Véresek vagyunk, de jót akarunk
stories.summary: Alföldi Róbert Julius Caesart rendezett, hogy mindenkit emlékeztessen: a hatalom természete nem változik, a demokrácia törékeny, a hamisan csengő jelszavak évezredek alatt sem koptak el. Nem árt résen lenni! Kritika.

stories.title: Will Ebola have an impact on the World Cup?
stories.summary: While ticket prices, heat and politics have made headlines, Ebola has been a World Cup worry for DR Congo. The African nation has qualified for the first time in 52 years, but does the outbreak have wider implications?

stories.title: Why Anthropic has the edge over OpenAI in IPO race
stories.summary: The rivalry between Sam Altman and Dario Amodei is entering a new phase. Both AI bosses are planning public listings and hope to use the money to outpace each other in the race to build superintelligent AI.

stories.title: From Russia to Israel to US, 'warlords' seize power — report
stories.summary: Russia invades Ukraine, Israel and the US attack Iran, civil wars drag on in Africa — and what about international law? German researchers paint a grim picture in their latest annual Peace Report.

stories.title: NASA picks first European astronaut for Artemis mission
stories.summary: Italian astronaut Luca Parmitano will be the lead pilot for the Artemis III test flight. The NASA mission is expected to launch in 2027 to test technology for a future moon landing.

stories.title: God's green Earth: US pastors preach environment stewardship
stories.summary: As the Trump administration undoes scores of environmental regulations and protections, some US Christian leaders are inviting their congregations to do as the Bible asks and be good stewards of the land.

stories.title: World Cup: Edin Dzeko still strong for Bosnia-Herzegovina
stories.summary: With so much unpleasantness surrounding this year's World Cup it's easy to forget that there's anything about it that can elicit a smile. But the story of Bosnia-Herzegovina captain Edin Dzeko sure can.

stories.title: Bear captured after roaming through Japanese city
stories.summary: Authorities in Utsunomiya, north of Tokyo, captured a bear after a multi-day search linked to several sightings. Security camera footage showed the animal running through a shopping arcade, prompting school closures and safety warnings.

stories.title: Top African referee denied entry into the US and will miss World Cup
stories.summary: A FIFA spokesperson confirmed in a statement that Artan, who says he was detained for 11 hours at Miami International Airport, would play no part in the World Cup.

stories.title: Hot air balloons fill Kazakhstan skies as festival returns
stories.summary: One of Kazakhstan's largest ballooning festivals returned to the Almaty region after a two-year break, bringing together 29 hot air balloons and pilots from 12 countries.

stories.title: Who is the Somali referee barred from entering the US for the World Cup?
stories.summary: Omar Artan was set to become the first Somali to referee a game at the World Cup finals, but his place in history has been denied by US immigration authorities.

stories.title: Spain: Thousands gather as Pope Leo XIV reaches Barcelona
stories.summary: Spain's Barcelona welcomed Pope Leo XIV on 9 June as thousands gathered for a prayer service at the city's cathedral of the Holy Cross and Saint Eulalia. The pontiff's visit includes a landmark ceremony at the Sagrada Familia Basilica.

stories.title: Nasa names next astronauts for Artemis Moon programme
stories.summary: Nasa names its next Artemis crew, though they will not be walking on the Moon or even going anywhere near it.

stories.title: Israeli air strikes hit Lebanese city of Tyre despite Iranian warning to stop attacks
stories.summary: Iran warned Israel on Monday that it could resume hostilities if attacks on its Lebanese ally Hezbollah do not stop.

stories.title: Does referee case show Fifa has lost control of its own World Cup?
stories.summary: With referee Omar Artan denied entry to the United States and worries over staff and supporters being turned back, what does this tell us about the World Cup?

stories.title: Murder of Lyhanna: Anger grows as thousands take to the streets in Paris
stories.summary: Thousands of people gathered in Paris to demand accountability and reforms after the killing of 11-year-old Lyhanna, criticising the handling of the case by French authorities and the justice system.

stories.title: Bowen: Trump and Netanyahu wanted to reshape the Middle East - now they risk a permacrisis
stories.summary: The US and Israeli leaders have lost control of the consequences after miscalculating the Iran war.

stories.title: Watch: BBC goes into jungle with Myanmar's civil war rebels
stories.summary: The BBC’s Quentin Sommerville travels to Myanmar - without the permission of the authorities - to meet a group of rebel fighters.

stories.title: Inside Myanmar, rebels are losing ground as military forces men into army
stories.summary: The BBC travels with rebels to frontline positions in Myanmar to see how the war is unfolding.

stories.title: Alleged Bondi Beach gunman charged with another 19 offences
stories.summary: Naveed Akram, 24, is already facing 59 charges over an attack on a Jewish festival in December.

stories.title: Exclusive: ‘If China attacks Taiwan, you will be affected too,’ Taiwan’s deputy FM warns Europe
stories.summary: Euronews Next sat down with Taiwan’s deputy foreign minister Wu Chih-chung in an exclusive interview to talk about the silicon shield and why the world cannot afford to ignore the political tensions.

stories.title: Spain: Human castle welcomes Pope Leo XIV to youth vigil in Barcelona
stories.summary: Welcomed by a towering Catalan human castle, Pope Leo XIV joined 40,000 young people in Barcelona on 9 June, urging greater support for those facing anxiety, loneliness and violence ahead of the blessing of the Sagrada Família's Tower of Jesus Christ.

stories.title: Former Air Canada captain accused of flying for years without the correct license
stories.summary: "Safety was not compromised by this incident because all pilots at Air Canada undergo mandatory recurrent training every six months to validate their flying competency," Air Canada said.

stories.title: USA: French Air Force display marks 250 years of American independence
stories.summary: France's renowned Patrouille de France performed a symbolic flyover above New York on 9 June. The display launched a series of events marking the upcoming 250th anniversary of the United States Declaration of Independence.

stories.title: Spain: Pope Leo XIV surprises passengers with cockpit visit on Madrid-Barcelona flight
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV made a rare visit to the cockpit mid-flight before greeting passengers and crew during his flight from Madrid to Barcelona on 9 June, marking the second stage of his week-long visit to Spain.

stories.title: Somali World Cup referee Omar Artan receives warm welcome home after being denied entry to US
stories.summary: Andrew Giuliani, who is leading the White House task force for the FIFA World Cup, has said Artan was denied entry to the US for a "very good reason," without offering further details.

stories.title: Volodymyr Zelenskyy reveals he wants to invite King Charles on a state visit to Ukraine – video
stories.summary: Ukrainian president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, tells the Guardian he was planning to invite the British monarch on a state visit to Ukraine as early as this year. Speaking to Pippa Crerar and Luke Harding, Zelenskyy says he has a close relationship with the king, who had continued to support Ukraine – but refuses to divulge exactly how Continue reading...

stories.title: Will Mexico's World Cup party be spoiled by teacher protest?
stories.summary: Just before the World Cup kicks off, host country Mexico faces a political tug-of-war. Striking teachers have occupied the central fan zone and have pledged to continue protesting.

stories.title: Belgium: Thousands protest alleged police violence in Brussels
stories.summary: Large crowds gathered in Brussels on 8 June to condemn what protesters described as excessive police force during recent demonstrations. The dispute is rooted in planned spending cuts affecting French-speaking education in Belgium

errors.error: <urlopen error [Errno -2] Name or service not known>
